Unai Simón.bio: Athletic Bilbao's commanding goalkeeper who established himself as Spain's number one during the Euro 2024 cycle. Born and raised in the Basque Country, he embodies the fighting spirit of Athletic Club and has grown into one of Europe's most reliable keepers.
Unai Simón.playingStyle: Commanding, athletic goalkeeper with excellent reflexes, brave shot-stopping, and improving distribution with his feet
keyStrengths: Command of the penalty area and aerial balls
Unai Simón.worldCup2026Role: Undisputed starting goalkeeper and a vocal leader organizing Spain's defensive line from behind
Unai Simón.notableFact: Recovered from a high-profile error in the Euro 2020 round of 16 against Croatia to become Spain's first-choice keeper

Dani Carvajal.bio: A Real Madrid legend and one of the most decorated right-backs in football history. Carvajal has won multiple Champions League titles and has been a cornerstone of both club and country for over a decade, combining defensive solidity with attacking thrust.
Dani Carvajal.playingStyle: Tenacious, experienced right-back who provides defensive steel and intelligent overlapping runs with pinpoint crosses
Dani Carvajal.worldCup2026Role: Veteran right-back bringing leadership and Champions League-level experience to the backline when fit
Dani Carvajal.notableFact: Has won six Champions League titles with Real Madrid, scoring in the 2024 final against Borussia Dortmund

Aymeric Laporte.bio: A French-born center-back who chose to represent Spain, Laporte was a key figure in their Euro 2024 triumph. After years at Manchester City under Pep Guardiola, he brings elite tactical awareness and ball-playing ability from the back.
Aymeric Laporte.playingStyle: Elegant, left-footed center-back who excels in possession-based systems with progressive passing and composure under pressure
keyStrengths: Aerial strength and composure on the ball
Aymeric Laporte.worldCup2026Role: Experienced center-back option bringing Guardiola-schooled tactical intelligence and left-footed balance to the defense
Aymeric Laporte.notableFact: Was born in France and played for French youth teams before switching allegiance to Spain, for whom he won Euro 2024

Nacho Fernández.bio: A Real Madrid academy product who spent his entire European career at the club, winning countless trophies including six Champions League titles. He moved to Saudi Arabia after Euro 2024 but remains a trusted option for Spain's national team.
Nacho Fernández.playingStyle: Versatile, reliable defender who can play across the backline and brings calm, experienced defending to any position
keyStrengths: Tactical versatility across all defensive positions
Calm, assured defending in high-pressure situations
Experience from the biggest matches in football
Nacho Fernández.worldCup2026Role: Veteran defensive utility player providing depth and experience across the backline in his likely final major tournament
Nacho Fernández.notableFact: Won six Champions League titles with Real Madrid while often serving as a squad player, earning the nickname 'Mr. Reliable'

Robin Le Normand.bio: A French-born center-back who grew up in Spain and developed at Real Sociedad before a big move to Atlético Madrid. His rugged defending and aerial prowess were key to Spain's Euro 2024 success, forming a strong partnership at the back.
Robin Le Normand.playingStyle: Physical, aggressive center-back who dominates aerial duels and is strong in one-on-one defending
keyStrengths: Dominant aerial presence at both ends
Consistency and reliability in a defensive partnership
Robin Le Normand.worldCup2026Role: Starting center-back forming the backbone of Spain's defense with his physical and aerial dominance
Robin Le Normand.notableFact: Born in France but raised in Spain since childhood, he qualifies through residency and chose to represent La Roja

Mikel Merino.bio: A tall, dynamic midfielder who moved from Real Sociedad to Arsenal, bringing his aerial ability and goalscoring threat from midfield to the Premier League. His headed goal against Germany in the Euro 2024 quarter-final is one of Spain's most iconic recent moments.
Mikel Merino.playingStyle: Box-to-box midfielder who combines height and aerial ability with technical quality, late runs into the box, and defensive work
keyStrengths: Aerial threat and goalscoring from midfield
Mikel Merino.worldCup2026Role: Starting midfielder providing aerial threat, energy, and the ability to contribute at both ends of the pitch
Mikel Merino.notableFact: Scored the dramatic stoppage-time winner against Germany in the Euro 2024 quarter-final with a towering header

Álvaro Morata.bio: Spain's captain and one of their most experienced internationals, Morata has played for Real Madrid, Juventus, Chelsea, Atlético Madrid, and AC Milan. Despite occasional criticism, his movement, link-up play, and international goal record are outstanding.
Álvaro Morata.playingStyle: Intelligent striker with excellent movement, strong link-up play, and clinical finishing who thrives on service from creative teammates
keyStrengths: Link-up play and selfless running for teammates
Álvaro Morata.worldCup2026Role: Captain and starting striker, leading the line with his experience, movement, and ability to bring others into play
Álvaro Morata.notableFact: Has scored in multiple Champions League finals and major tournament knockout stages, establishing himself as a big-game player for Spain

Fabián Ruiz.bio: A silky midfielder who emerged at Real Betis and Napoli before joining Paris Saint-Germain. His performances at Euro 2024, where he was named in the Team of the Tournament, confirmed his status as one of Europe's premier central midfielders.
Fabián Ruiz.playingStyle: Elegant, technically refined midfielder who controls the tempo with smooth passing, dribbling through the lines, and creative vision
keyStrengths: Silky ball control and dribbling through pressure
Fabián Ruiz.worldCup2026Role: Starting midfielder orchestrating play from the center, providing technical quality and creativity through the middle third
Fabián Ruiz.notableFact: Was named in the Euro 2024 Team of the Tournament after a series of outstanding displays that drove Spain's title-winning campaign

Joselu Mato.bio: A journeyman striker who played in England and across Spain before a fairytale spell at Real Madrid where he scored crucial Champions League goals. His perseverance through years in football's lower tiers before reaching the pinnacle is remarkable.
Joselu Mato.playingStyle: Experienced target man with good aerial ability, intelligent positioning, and a poacher's instinct inside the box
keyStrengths: Experience and composure in front of goal
Joselu Mato.worldCup2026Role: Experienced backup striker providing an aerial and physical alternative when Spain need a different approach in attack
Joselu Mato.notableFact: Scored two goals as a substitute in Real Madrid's dramatic Champions League semi-final comeback against Bayern Munich in 2024

Dani Olmo.bio: A uniquely developed talent who spent his formative years at Dinamo Zagreb in Croatia rather than in Spain, which gave him a distinctive edge. His creativity, goalscoring, and big-game performances at Euro 2024 were pivotal to Spain's title triumph.
Dani Olmo.playingStyle: Creative, versatile attacker who can play as a number 10 or on the wing, combining vision, dribbling, and goalscoring threat
keyStrengths: Versatility to play multiple attacking positions
Dani Olmo.worldCup2026Role: Starting attacking midfielder and creative hub, tasked with unlocking defenses and contributing goals in the final third
Dani Olmo.notableFact: Won the Euro 2024 Golden Boot (joint) and was named Player of the Tournament for his decisive performances throughout the competition

Ferran Torres.bio: A quick, direct attacker who established himself at Valencia before spells at Manchester City and Barcelona. His international record is exceptional relative to his age, and he has consistently delivered for Spain in major tournaments.
Ferran Torres.playingStyle: Direct, pacey forward who makes intelligent runs in behind, finishes clinically, and can play across the front line
keyStrengths: Pace and intelligent runs behind defenses
Ferran Torres.worldCup2026Role: Rotation attacker providing pace, directness, and a proven international goal threat from either wing or through the middle
Ferran Torres.notableFact: Has one of the best goals-per-game ratios in Spain's history, scoring consistently in international football from a young age

David Raya.bio: A Spanish goalkeeper who built his career in England's lower leagues with Blackburn and Brentford before joining Arsenal, where he became the Premier League's best goalkeeper. His rise from the Championship to elite-level football is a story of perseverance.
David Raya.playingStyle: Modern goalkeeper who excels at distribution, sweeper-keeper actions, and penalty saving, thriving in a high-defensive-line system
keyStrengths: Exceptional distribution and playing out from the back
David Raya.worldCup2026Role: High-quality backup goalkeeper pushing Unai Simón for the starting spot, bringing Premier League experience and elite distribution
David Raya.notableFact: Won the Premier League Golden Glove at Arsenal and has one of the best penalty-saving records in English football

Alejandro Grimaldo.bio: A Barcelona academy graduate who found his breakthrough at Benfica in Portugal before joining Bayer Leverkusen, where he was instrumental in their unbeaten Bundesliga title. His offensive output from left-back is among the best in world football.
Alejandro Grimaldo.playingStyle: Attack-minded left-back with exceptional crossing, set-piece delivery, and the ability to score spectacular free kicks
Alejandro Grimaldo.worldCup2026Role: Starting left-back providing world-class offensive output, set-piece expertise, and width in Spain's attacking system
Alejandro Grimaldo.notableFact: Registered over 15 goal contributions in Leverkusen's unbeaten Bundesliga season, unprecedented numbers for a full-back

Rodri Hernández.bio: The 2024 Ballon d'Or winner and the heartbeat of both Manchester City and Spain. Rodri's importance is so immense that City's form visibly drops without him, and his metronomic presence was the foundation of Spain's Euro 2024 triumph.
Rodri Hernández.playingStyle: Complete defensive midfielder who controls the game's rhythm with precise passing, intelligent positioning, and an elite reading of the game
keyStrengths: Exceptional game control and tempo setting
Rodri Hernández.worldCup2026Role: The irreplaceable midfield anchor and Spain's most important player, controlling the game from deep and setting the team's rhythm
Rodri Hernández.notableFact: Won the 2024 Ballon d'Or, becoming the first defensive midfielder to win the award in over a decade, and went on a 70+ match unbeaten run with City

Marc Cucurella.bio: An energetic left-back who developed at Barcelona's La Masia before playing for Getafe, Brighton, and Chelsea. His tireless running and defensive tenacity made him a fan favorite at Euro 2024 despite the controversy around handball incidents.
Marc Cucurella.playingStyle: Energetic, aggressive left-back who defends with intensity, covers every blade of grass, and provides attacking width
keyStrengths: Versatility to play left-back or left wing-back
Marc Cucurella.worldCup2026Role: Rotation option at left-back bringing boundless energy, defensive intensity, and the ability to cover the entire left flank
Marc Cucurella.notableFact: Became a viral sensation at Euro 2024, with his distinctive curly hair and spirited celebrations endearing him to fans worldwide

Pedri González.bio: A midfield maestro who joined Barcelona as a teenager from Las Palmas and immediately looked like he belonged at the highest level. Despite battling injuries, his talent is undeniable -- smooth passing, intelligent movement, and the ability to control matches from central midfield.
Pedri González.playingStyle: Technically exquisite midfielder who plays with a maturity beyond his years, combining smooth passing, press resistance, and spatial awareness
keyStrengths: Press resistance and ability to receive under pressure
Smooth, accurate passing and ball retention
Pedri González.worldCup2026Role: Starting central midfielder forming the creative engine alongside Rodri, controlling possession and threading passes through the lines
Pedri González.notableFact: Was named the Best Young Player at Euro 2020 at just 18 years old, drawing comparisons to Andrés Iniesta

Nico Williams.bio: A explosive winger from Athletic Bilbao who lit up Euro 2024 with his pace, directness, and fearless dribbling. Born to Ghanaian parents who walked across the Sahara to reach Spain, his story is one of the most inspiring in modern football.
Nico Williams.playingStyle: Explosive, direct winger with blistering pace, fearless one-on-one dribbling, and the ability to score and create in equal measure
keyStrengths: End product with both goals and assists
Nico Williams.worldCup2026Role: Starting left winger and one of Spain's primary attacking weapons, tasked with stretching defenses and creating chances with his pace
Nico Williams.notableFact: His parents walked across the Sahara Desert from Ghana to reach Spain, and his brother Iñaki plays internationally for Ghana

Martín Zubimendi.bio: A one-club man at Real Sociedad who turned down offers from top European clubs out of loyalty to his hometown team. His elegant reading of the game and ability to dictate play from the base of midfield have drawn comparisons to Spanish midfield legends.
Martín Zubimendi.playingStyle: Elegant, deep-lying playmaker who shields the defense with intelligent positioning and distributes the ball with metronomic precision
keyStrengths: Elite positional awareness and game reading
Press resistance and composure on the ball
Martín Zubimendi.worldCup2026Role: Key backup to Rodri in the defensive midfield role, providing a seamless stylistic replacement with his similar skillset
Martín Zubimendi.notableFact: Famously turned down a move to Liverpool despite agreeing personal terms, choosing to stay loyal to his boyhood club Real Sociedad

Lamine Yamal.bio: The youngest player to score at a European Championship, Yamal is a generational talent who debuted for Barcelona at 15 and Spain at 16. His precocious ability, fearless mentality, and dazzling skill have made him one of the most exciting players on the planet.
Lamine Yamal.playingStyle: Prodigiously talented right winger who combines outrageous close control, creative vision, and a devastating left foot far beyond his teenage years
keyStrengths: Devastating left foot for shots and crosses
Lamine Yamal.worldCup2026Role: Starting right winger and Spain's most electrifying talent, the player opponents fear most and who can single-handedly change matches
Lamine Yamal.notableFact: Became the youngest ever scorer at a European Championship at Euro 2024 with a stunning goal against France in the semi-final at age 16

Jesús Navas.bio: A legendary Spanish winger-turned-right-back who has been representing his country for nearly two decades. A 2010 World Cup and Euro 2012 winner, Navas is one of Spain's most capped players and a symbol of loyalty to his hometown club Sevilla.
Jesús Navas.playingStyle: Experienced, tireless right-back with electric pace that defies his age, strong defensive positioning, and a veteran's reading of the game
keyStrengths: Remarkable pace maintained at veteran age
Jesús Navas.worldCup2026Role: Senior squad member and emergency right-back option, bringing unmatched tournament experience and leadership in what would be a farewell appearance
Jesús Navas.notableFact: Won the 2010 World Cup, Euro 2012, and two Europa League titles with Sevilla, and has battled a well-known phobia of traveling away from home

Mikel Oyarzabal.bio: Real Sociedad's captain and a player who has delivered in Spain's biggest moments, including scoring the winning goal in the Euro 2024 final. His intelligence, timing of runs, and ability to score crucial goals make him invaluable to the national team.
Mikel Oyarzabal.playingStyle: Intelligent, versatile forward who makes clever runs, times his movement perfectly, and scores decisive goals in big moments
keyStrengths: Versatility to play on either wing or as a striker
Mikel Oyarzabal.worldCup2026Role: Impact substitute and big-game player who can change matches with his movement and finishing, especially in knockout stages
Mikel Oyarzabal.notableFact: Scored the winning goal in the Euro 2024 final against England, cementing his status as one of Spain's most clutch players

Pau Torres.bio: A left-footed center-back who developed at Villarreal and helped them win the Europa League before moving to Aston Villa in the Premier League. His elegance on the ball and composure in possession make him ideal for Spain's playing philosophy.
Pau Torres.playingStyle: Elegant, left-footed center-back who excels at playing out from the back with progressive passing and composed defending
keyStrengths: Composure in possession and build-up play
Ability to carry the ball forward from deep
Pau Torres.worldCup2026Role: Rotation center-back providing left-footed balance and ball-playing quality when Spain need to dominate possession from the back
Pau Torres.notableFact: Was a key part of Villarreal's historic Europa League triumph in 2021, defeating Manchester United in the final on penalties

Álex Remiro.bio: Real Sociedad's reliable goalkeeper who has been a consistent performer in La Liga for several years. His steady presence between the posts and loyalty to his club have earned him a place in Spain's deep goalkeeping pool.
Álex Remiro.playingStyle: Steady, reliable goalkeeper with good shot-stopping, consistent performances, and calm handling of crosses
keyStrengths: Calm handling and command of the area
Álex Remiro.worldCup2026Role: Third-choice goalkeeper providing experienced depth and a steady training partner for the first and second-choice keepers
Álex Remiro.notableFact: Was part of the Real Sociedad side that ended their 34-year wait for the Copa del Rey title in 2020

Gavi Páez.bio: A fearless, fiery midfielder who burst into Barcelona's first team as a 17-year-old and immediately established himself as a key player. His aggressive pressing, tenacity, and technical skill earned him the Golden Boy award in 2022, and he has been rebuilding after a serious knee injury.
Gavi Páez.playingStyle: Fiery, aggressive midfielder who presses relentlessly, competes for every ball, and combines tenacity with surprising technical quality
Gavi Páez.worldCup2026Role: Midfield dynamo providing energy, aggression, and pressing intensity as a starter or impactful substitute
Gavi Páez.notableFact: Won the 2022 Golden Boy award and became one of the youngest players to represent Spain, debuting at 17 years and 62 days

Ayoze Pérez.bio: A versatile attacker who spent years in the Premier League with Newcastle and Leicester before returning to Spain with Villarreal, where he rediscovered his best form. His experience and ability to play multiple forward positions add valuable depth to Spain's squad.
Ayoze Pérez.playingStyle: Intelligent, versatile forward who can play across the attacking line, combining clever movement with clinical finishing
Ayoze Pérez.worldCup2026Role: Experienced squad player providing attacking depth and versatility across the forward line from the bench
Ayoze Pérez.notableFact: Scored a memorable hat-trick for Newcastle against Southampton in the Premier League during his time in England

Dani Vivian.bio: An Athletic Bilbao center-back who has risen through the ranks at the Basque club to become a dependable defender in La Liga. His partnership with other Athletic players in the national team setup gives Spain a well-drilled defensive option.
Dani Vivian.playingStyle: Solid, committed center-back who defends aggressively, wins aerial duels, and brings a warrior mentality to the backline
keyStrengths: Strong understanding with Athletic Bilbao teammates
Dani Vivian.worldCup2026Role: Defensive depth option at center-back, providing cover and a physical presence when needed in the squad
Dani Vivian.notableFact: Has been part of Athletic Bilbao's resurgence in recent seasons, helping them compete consistently in the top half of La Liga
